_note: GENUINE news-derived seeds for MP (MP Materials) — real events, real URLs. ENTRY: China's Apr-4-2025 rare-earth export controls (Announcement 18) choking a supply chain the US depends on China for ~70%, with MP the lone scaled US producer at Mountain Pass. ACCELERATION: the Jul-2025 DoD public-private partnership ($400M equity, Pentagon becomes largest shareholder, 10-yr $110/kg NdPr price floor) that sent MP ~+50% in a day and ~+85% in July. Replaces the prior synthetic MP seed. Look-ahead caveat: curator training likely postdates these dates (upper bound, non-negotiable #4/#6). Non-blank url = news-derived.

articles.title: China imposes export controls on medium and heavy rare earths - MP Materials the lone scaled US producer
articles.snippet: China's Ministry of Commerce and General Administration of Customs issued Announcement 18 on April 4, imposing export controls on seven medium and heavy rare-earth materials - samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium and yttrium - and the permanent magnets made from them, choking a supply chain the US relies on China for roughly 70%. MP Materials, which mines and processes rare earths at Mountain Pass, California, is the only scaled US producer positioned to benefit - still under-owned as the curbs are only just biting.

articles.title: Pentagon to become largest shareholder in rare-earth miner MP Materials; shares surge 50%
articles.snippet: The Department of Defense agreed to purchase $400 million of a new series of MP Materials preferred stock, positioning DoD to become the company's largest shareholder. The 10-year agreement establishes a price-floor commitment of $110 per kilogram for MP's neodymium-praseodymium (NdPr) output, ensuring stable and predictable cash flow. Shares soared about 50% to close at $45.23; the stock rose roughly 85% in July and is up about 365% year to date.
